topic: Automation Rules Engines for Personal Agent OS - Event-Driven Reactive Systems

sources.title: expr-lang/expr: Go Expression Language
keyInsights: Safe expression evaluation: memory-safe, side-effect-free, always terminates
Bytecode VM with optimizing compiler for high performance
Compile-time type checking with Go struct integration
Used by Google, Uber, ByteDance, Kubernetes projects
Built-in functions: all, none, any, one, filter, map

sources.title: expr-lang/expr Go Package API Reference
keyInsights: Compile() returns thread-safe vm.Program for reuse
AsBooll() enforces boolean output at compile time
AllowUndefinedVariables() for flexible runtime environments
MaxNodes() limits expression complexity for security
Custom functions via Function() and operator overloading via Operator()

sources.title: expr-lang Getting Started Guide
keyInsights: Compile once, run many pattern for performance
Programs are safe for concurrent use
Struct environments expose methods as callable functions
Type validation at compile time prevents runtime surprises

sources.title: CEL-Go: Common Expression Language for Go
keyInsights: Linear time evaluation, mutation-free, not Turing-complete
Partial state evaluation handles missing variables gracefully
Thread-safe compiled programs, cacheable for reuse
Apache 2.0 license, 2.9k stars, used by 14.2k projects
First-class protobuf support with gradual typing

sources.title: CEL Specification: Common Expression Language
keyInsights: C-like syntax familiar to most developers
Designed for security policy evaluation
Canonical protocol buffer serialization for persistence
Extensible with application-defined functions

sources.title: Home Assistant Automation Triggers
keyInsights: 20+ trigger types: state, numeric, time, event, MQTT, webhook, template, zone, sun, tag, calendar
Trigger IDs enable conditional routing in actions
for: duration option delays trigger until condition holds for specified time
Multiple triggers per automation, any one fires the rule
Trigger variables accessible via trigger_variables block

sources.title: Home Assistant Automation Conditions
keyInsights: Conditions check current state, not events - critical distinction from triggers
All conditions must be true (AND logic by default)
OR/NOT conditions for complex logic
Template conditions with Jinja2 for arbitrary expressions

sources.title: Home Assistant Automation Actions
keyInsights: Actions are scripts - supports service calls, delays, conditions, scenes
Conditional logic within actions halts execution if false
Actions compose as lists for multi-step execution

sources.title: Home Assistant YAML Automation Format
keyInsights: Four execution modes: single, restart, queued, parallel
max parameter limits concurrent executions in queued/parallel mode
Trace configuration for execution debugging (stored_traces)
initial_state controls startup behavior

sources.title: GitHub Actions Event Triggers
keyInsights: 30+ event types with activity type filters
Branch/tag/path filters combine as AND conditions
repository_dispatch and workflow_dispatch for custom/manual triggers
Three levels of workflow nesting maximum
Scheduled workflows disable after 60 days inactivity

sources.title: GitHub Actions Expression Syntax
keyInsights: Expression syntax: ${{ }} with context objects, operators, functions
Status functions: success(), failure(), always(), cancelled()
Object filter syntax: collection.*.property returns array
Case-insensitive string comparison, loose type coercion
Functions: contains, startsWith, endsWith, format, join, toJSON, fromJSON

sources.title: GitHub Actions Workflow Commands
keyInsights: Environment file pattern: GITHUB_ENV, GITHUB_OUTPUT, GITHUB_STATE
Annotations: debug, notice, warning, error with file positioning
Job summaries support Markdown, 1MiB limit per step
Multiline values via delimiter pattern

sources.title: IFTTT Service API Architecture
keyInsights: Polling triggers: hourly check, return 50 most recent events
Realtime triggers: service POSTs to realtime.ifttt.com for instant execution
Trigger ingredients flow into action fields via template
Trigger identity enables subscription management
Health check endpoint for service availability monitoring

sources.title: n8n Workflow Architecture
keyInsights: Three node categories: Core, Action (500+ integrations), Cluster (AI)
Trigger types: manual, schedule, webhook, app-specific, email IMAP
IF/Switch/Filter nodes for conditional branching
Expression language with JMESPath for complex JSON queries
Export/import and version control support

sources.title: n8n Error Handling Model
keyInsights: Error Trigger node captures workflow failures
Stop And Error node for intentional failure
Dedicated error workflows for exception management
Sub-workflows for specialized error handling

sources.title: n8n Looping Patterns
keyInsights: Loop Over Items (Split in Batches) for iterative processing
Process items individually or in batches
Conditional looping and completion-based looping
Item linking preserves data relationships across loop iterations

sources.title: Inngest Event Triggers
keyInsights: Events trigger functions from backend, cron, webhooks, or other functions
Event filtering, delayed execution, batching, multiple triggers
Fan-out: one event triggers multiple functions simultaneously
Cross-application function triggering capability

sources.title: Inngest Steps and Workflows
keyInsights: Steps as retriable checkpoints for fault tolerance
step.run() for named operations with data reuse
Flow control: concurrency, throttling, temporal distribution
Durable execution with step-level error handling

sources.title: Inngest Function Creation API
keyInsights: CEL expressions for event filtering and concurrency keys
Concurrency: limit, scope (fn/env/account), key for virtual queues
Throttling: limit per period with burst support
Debouncing: configurable period with scoped keys
Cancellation via cancelOn with event matching and CEL conditions
Event batching: maxSize (100), timeout, conditional batching

sources.title: Inngest Error Handling
keyInsights: Error vs failure distinction: error triggers retry, failure is permanent
Failure handlers as callbacks when all retries exhausted
Individual step retry logic and rollback support
Idempotency requirement for safe retries

sources.title: How Temporal Works: Durable Execution
keyInsights: Workflow state is durable and fault-tolerant by default
Activities separate failure-prone operations from workflow logic
Workers poll task queues, alternative workers resume on crash
Scales beyond 200M executions/second
Event history enables deterministic replay for state recovery

sources.title: Temporal Workflow Engine Design Principles
keyInsights: Deterministic replay preserves complete program state including local variables
Event-driven state machine: state in -> commands out -> execute -> notify -> repeat
Task queues for activity routing, flow control, load distribution
Transfer queues: local shard queues with async transfer (transactional outbox pattern)
Sharding by workflow ID with consistent hashing
Transactional consistency across DB, timers, and task queues is critical

sources.title: Temporal Go SDK: Core Application Patterns
keyInsights: Workflow functions: workflow.Context as first param, serializable params only
Activity options: StartToCloseTimeout, retry policies with backoff
Deterministic constraints: use workflow.Now(), workflow.Sleep(), workflow.Go()
Dynamic workflows/activities via converter.EncodedValues
Worker configuration: max concurrent activities, activities per second

sources.title: Temporal Go Message Passing
keyInsights: Queries: synchronous, read-only state inspection
Signals: asynchronous messages that modify workflow behavior
Updates: synchronous with validators, can modify state and return results
workflow.Mutex for safe concurrent handler access
Signals persist in Event History for durability

sources.title: Windmill Architecture Overview
keyInsights: Multi-language support: TypeScript, Python, Go, PHP, Bash, C#, SQL, Rust
12+ trigger types: schedule, webhook, email, HTTP, Kafka, NATS, SQS, MQTT, Postgres
Orchestrator for low-code or YAML flow assembly
Reduces boilerplate for UIs, error handling, scaling, dependencies

sources.title: Windmill Flow Triggers
keyInsights: Trigger scripts pull data from external sources and return new items since last run
State system: persistent JSON objects as resources to track processed items
Data flows from triggers into for loops for batch processing
Native triggers for Postgres, Kafka, NATS, SQS, MQTT, GCP, WebSocket

sources.title: Windmill Error Handling in Flows
keyInsights: Error handler: special step that executes on flow error
Receives input from failed step including error details
Activates after retry attempts exhausted
Complementary: suspend/approval, early stop, early return

sources.title: Martin Fowler: Event-Driven Architecture Patterns
keyInsights: Four distinct patterns: event notification, event-carried state transfer, event sourcing, CQRS
These patterns are often conflated, causing architectural confusion
Event notification: simple, decoupled, but complex flows hard to track
Event-carried state transfer: reduced latency, improved resilience, significant duplication
CQRS is orthogonal to events, works independently

sources.title: Event Sourcing Pattern (microservices.io)
keyInsights: Event store acts as both database and message broker
State reconstructed by replaying events in sequence
Snapshots for performance with long event histories
Events are immutable, append-only operations
Concurrency and ordering must be maintained per aggregate

sources.title: Event Sourcing Pattern (Microsoft Azure)
keyInsights: Append-only store as system of record, materialized views for queries
Event versioning challenges: handlers must support all versions
Snapshots at intervals reduce replay cost
Idempotency requirement for event consumers
Circular logic warning: event processing creating new events can cause infinite loops

sources.title: Saga Pattern (Microsoft Azure)
keyInsights: Choreography: services exchange events without centralized controller
Orchestration: centralized orchestrator directs participants via commands
Three transaction types: compensable, pivot (point of no return), retryable
Data anomalies: lost updates, dirty reads, fuzzy reads
Countermeasures: semantic lock, commutative updates, pessimistic view, reread values

sources.title: Saga Pattern (microservices.io)
keyInsights: Sequence of local transactions with compensating transactions on failure
Choreography: services publish domain events autonomously
Orchestration: central orchestrator sends commands and receives replies
Must atomically update database AND publish messages
Three client outcome approaches: await, poll, or event notification

sources.title: Circuit Breaker Pattern (Microsoft Azure)
keyInsights: Three states: Closed (normal), Open (blocking), Half-Open (testing recovery)
Time-based failure counter with automatic reset
Success counter in Half-Open state for controlled recovery
Manual override for administrator control
Accelerated tripping via failure response content (e.g., HTTP 429)
Different purpose from Retry pattern - prevents likely-to-fail operations

sources.title: OPA/Rego Policy Language
keyInsights: Declarative policy language inspired by Datalog
Rules as virtual documents with head + body structure
Comprehensions for building collections: array, object, set
Universal quantification via every keyword, negation, or comprehensions
Default values for fallback when conditions not met
Metadata annotations for tooling and type checking

sources.title: OPA Integration Patterns
keyInsights: Four integration methods: REST API, Go SDK, Go rego package, WebAssembly
SDK provides simplified API with bundle management and decision logging
Wasm offers less overhead than REST, potentially better than native Go eval
Bundle system for distributing policies to instances

sources.title: Grule Rule Engine for Go
keyInsights: Drools-inspired GRL syntax with when/then blocks
Salience attribute controls rule execution priority
Performance: 0.009ms per evaluation against 100 rules
Direct struct field access without getter/setter methods
Inference Engine with Rete-style evaluation

sources.title: GoRules Zen: Business Rules Engine
keyInsights: JSON Decision Model (JDM) with interconnected graph nodes
Decision tables: row-by-row AND evaluation with hit policies (first/collect)
Five node types: decision table, switch, function, expression, decision
Cross-language: Rust core with Go, Node.js, Python bindings
Function nodes with 50ms timeout limit for safety

sources.title: HashiCorp go-bexpr: Boolean Expression Evaluator
keyInsights: Boolean expression evaluation against Go structs and maps
Struct tags control field exposure (bexpr:'-' excludes fields)
Uses pointerstructure for nested data access
Simple operators: == and != comparisons

sources.title: nikunjy/rules: Go Rules Engine
keyInsights: ANTLR4-based parser for rule expressions
SCIM-like operators: eq, ne, lt, gt, co, sw, ew, in, pr
Evaluates against map[string]interface{} data
Nested property access with dot notation
Case-insensitive operations

sources.title: Gval: Go Expression Evaluator
keyInsights: Go-like expression syntax with all standard operators
Performance: 3.5ns constant, 165-352ns parameter evaluation
JSON Path support via jsonpath extension
Custom functions, operators, and constants
Parse once, evaluate many pattern

sources.title: Govaluate: Go Expression Evaluation (Archived)
keyInsights: ARCHIVED as of March 2025 - do not use for new projects
Parse time ~1.4us, eval time ~30ns - good benchmark reference
Full operator set including regex matching
Custom functions with variable arguments

sources.title: Goja: ECMAScript Runtime in Pure Go
keyInsights: Full ECMAScript 5.1 with most ES6 features
Not goroutine-safe - requires single-threaded access per Runtime
Bidirectional Go-JS value conversion via ToValue/Export
Pure Go, no cgo dependency
Useful for full scripting when simple expressions insufficient

sources.title: SQLite CREATE TRIGGER Documentation
keyInsights: BEFORE/AFTER/INSTEAD OF trigger timing
WHEN clause for conditional trigger execution
NEW/OLD references for accessing row data in triggers
RAISE() function for error handling (ROLLBACK, ABORT, FAIL, IGNORE)
Use cases: referential integrity, audit logging, cascading operations

sources.title: robfig/cron: Go Cron Scheduler
keyInsights: Standard and Quartz cron formats with custom parser options
CRON_TZ for timezone support
Job wrapping via Chain: panic recovery, delayed execution, skip on running
V3 supports job removal for lifecycle management
Functional options pattern for scheduler construction

sources.title: gojq: Pure Go jq Implementation
keyInsights: Full jq query language for JSON transformation
Embeddable as Go library with custom functions
Arbitrary-precision integer arithmetic
WithFunction/WithIterFunction for custom extensions
Useful for complex JSON event data extraction in rules

sources.title: Langflow: Visual AI Builder
keyInsights: Visual drag-and-drop flow builder with Python backend
Agent fleet with components as tools
Flow-as-API deployment for production use
Pre-built flows and components library

sources.title: Flowise: AI Agent Builder
keyInsights: Agentflow for multi-agent orchestration, Chatflow for single agents
Human-in-the-loop for agent task review
REST API, embedded chat, TypeScript/Python SDKs
Observable execution with Prometheus/OpenTelemetry
